title: Can I download the code in a scrim?
description: Certainly! Navigate to the project/lesson you'd like to download, hit the cog icon in the bottom right corner, and choose Download as Zip. Please note that some
body:
Support Home
Can I download the code in a scrim?
Certainly! Navigate to the project/lesson you'd like to download, hit the cog icon in the bottom right corner, and choose Download as Zip.
Please note that some Scrimba projects are bundled in a special way in order to make them run fully in the browser, which means you're not guaranteed that your project will run as might you expect it will do locally. This is usually the case with projects that use NPM modules, as they are usually not something you'd be able to run without Node JS. So in order to make it work in the browser, we've had to do some magic under the hood on Scrimba.com.
Last updated on August 2, 2024

title: Do I keep my progress even if I cancel my subscription?
description: Yes! All your progress (both for our free and premium content) will be saved regardless of your subscription status. If you decide to cancel and then return lat
body:
Support Home
Do I keep my progress even if I cancel my subscription?
Yes! All your progress (both for our free and premium content) will be saved regardless of your subscription status. If you decide to cancel and then return later down the line, you'll continue where you previously left off.

title: Do your lessons have captions?
description: Yes, it does! Our system has native built-in captions for our lessons. Captions can be controlled by the "CC" button located in the lower left of the lesson env
body:
Support Home
Do your lessons have captions?
Yes, it does! Our system has native built-in captions for our lessons. Captions can be controlled by the "CC" button located in the lower left of the lesson environment. If you encounter any lessons that do not have captions, please notify us.

Currently, captions are only available in English. We are working to develop multi-language caption support. We also don't have any transcripts for our lesson at this time.
Last updated on February 13, 2025

title: My automatic payment failed. What do I do?
description: If your subscription payment failed (e.g. you didn't have funds in your account when we attempted to charge your card), you will lose access to the Pro courses.
body:
Support Home
My automatic payment failed. What do I do?
If your subscription payment failed (e.g. you didn't have funds in your account when we attempted to charge your card), you will lose access to the Pro courses. An automatic attempt to recharge your card will occur within a couple of days. After several failures, your subscription will be put on hold.

Please contact us at if you encounter any problems with payment failures, and we will assist you in resolving this issue.
Last updated on December 9, 2024

title: I get "Email is Already Registered" error when trying to access Discord
description: If you're encountering the "Email is Already Registered" error on Discord, this typically means that there's already an account associated with the email addres
body:
Support Home
I get "Email is Already Registered" error when trying to access Discord

If you're encountering the "Email is Already Registered" error on Discord, this typically means that there's already an account associated with the email address you're attempting to use. This situation can arise if you've previously set up an account with that email or if it has been mistakenly or intentionally used by someone else.

To address this issue, you may follow these steps:
Password Reset: If you've forgotten about an account you created with this email, you can reset your password. Visit the Discord password reset page, input your email address, and adhere to the instructions sent to your email. This step will help you regain access to your existing account.
Alternate Email: Should the password reset not resolve your issue, or if you suspect your email is being used without your consent, you might consider employing an alternate email address to create your Discord account.
Support Contact: If you find yourself still facing issues, it's important to note that this is not a matter we can assist with directly. For further help, you must reach out to Discord's support team directly.

For more comprehensive guidance and additional details, please refer to the official Discord support documentation here: https://support.discord.com/hc/en-us/articles/360043677651--Email-is-Already-Registered-Errors.
Last updated on December 10, 2024
Related Articles

title: How do I manage my subscription/billing account/invoice?
description: Adding your name, address, tax ID, etc., to your billing account and invoice Navigate to your account's settings page, and click on "View billing". This will op
body:
Support Home
How do I manage my subscription/billing account/invoice?
Adding your name, address, tax ID, etc., to your billing account and invoice
Navigate to your account's settings page, and click on "View billing".
This will open up your billing portal where you can update your billing account information.
Updated information will only appear on future invoices and receipts. To have them appear on past transactions, simply redownload them via your billing portal.

Updating your card details
Navigate to your account's settings page, and click on "View billing".
This will open up your billing portal where you can access your card details.

Cancelling your subscription
Click your profile name in the top left of the website.
Navigate to the Settings menu.
Locate the Subscriptions section.
To cancel, click on the "x" icon located on the right.
To manage your billing details, click on "View billing".
Last updated on April 26, 2025

How do I change the name of my certificate?

title: How do I get the Student Github free access?
description: With the GitHub Student Developer Pack, you’ll enjoy 1 free month of Scrimba Pro, giving you full access to all our courses and exclusive Pro channels on Discor
body:
Support Home
How do I get the Student Github free access?
With the GitHub Student Developer Pack, you’ll enjoy 1 free month of Scrimba Pro, giving you full access to all our courses and exclusive Pro channels on Discord.

Sign Up for the Student Developer Pack
Visit the GitHub Education benefits page at https://education.github.com/pack
Follow the instructions to apply for the GitHub Student Developer Pack.
Wait for GitHub to approve your application. This process may take some time.

Activate 30-days off your subscription
Log into Scrimba via your Github
If GitHub recognizes your student status, the free one-month pack will be activated.
There will be a yellow "UPGRADE" button located in the lower left.
In the checkout modal, there should be a message noting that you have applied for the "GitHub Student 30-Day Trial"

Still having issues?
Try logging out, and then log back in after your Student Developer back has been activated. This will trigger a fresh request to verify your student status if it's not detecting it.
Make sure your GitHub Student Developer Pack is active and correctly linked to your correct GitHub account.
Last updated on September 27, 2025

title: I see red error lines in my Typescript code
description: You may encounter red squiggly lines when using TypeScript code even when the syntax and code context are correct. We’re aware of this issue and are actively wo
body:
Support Home
I see red error lines in my Typescript code
You may encounter red squiggly lines when using TypeScript code even when the syntax and code context are correct. We’re aware of this issue and are actively working to resolve it. We understand that this can be confusing, especially when you’re learning, and we appreciate your patience as we address the problem.

If your code runs correctly despite the red squiggly lines, you can safely disregard them for now. To determine whether there’s a real error in your code:
Run your code to see if it produces the correct output and behaves as expected.
If there is a legitimate issue, the code will throw an error when executed.

We apologize for any confusion this may cause and thank you for your understanding as we work to improve your experience on our platform.
Last updated on December 10, 2024

title: Mobile and tablet compatibility with Scrimba
description: Our platform is designed to provide the best learning experience on desktop and laptop devices. While it may be accessible on mobile phones and some tablets, ce
body:
Support Home
Mobile and tablet compatibility with Scrimba
Our platform is designed to provide the best learning experience on desktop and laptop devices. While it may be accessible on mobile phones and some tablets, certain features may not function as intended due to compatibility issues.

Why Desktop/Laptop is Recommended:
• Full feature accessibility: Some tools and features may not display or work correctly on smaller screens.
• Improved usability: A larger screen allows for easier navigation and coding, especially for lessons that require intricate interactions.

Mobile and Tablet Limitations:
• Certain interactions, such as drag-and-drop or code editing, may not work seamlessly.
• Limited screen size can hinder visibility and usability.
• Compatibility may vary depending on the device and browser used.

To ensure a smooth and uninterrupted learning experience, we strongly recommend using a desktop or laptop device. If you encounter issues while using a mobile or tablet, try switching to a desktop browser for the best performance.

Thank you for your understanding, and we’re always working to improve compatibility across all devices!
Last updated on February 5, 2025
Related Articles
My code crashes or freezes the browser

title: My code crashes or freezes the browser
description: If your code is causing the browser to crash or freeze, it’s likely due to an issue in the code itself. Our platform operates as a coding sandbox, so certain er
body:
Support Home
My code crashes or freezes the browser
If your code is causing the browser to crash or freeze, it’s likely due to an issue in the code itself. Our platform operates as a coding sandbox, so certain errors can overload your browser.
Common Causes:
Infinite Loops: Loops without a proper exit condition can run indefinitely, using up all your browser’s resources.
Runaway Code: Overloading your browser with excessive tasks, like creating too many elements or recursive calls without a stopping condition, can also cause issues.
How to Resolve It:
Create a New Scrim/Scribble: Start fresh in a new scrim/scribble and test smaller portions of your code to identify the problem.
Revert to a Previous Version: Use a saved instance of your code (if available) to revert to a working version and amend the issue from there.
Close and Restart the Browser: If your browser freezes, restart it to regain functionality before troubleshooting further.
By revising your code carefully and testing in smaller increments, you can identify and fix problematic areas. If the issue persists, feel free to contact us for support.
Last updated on February 5, 2025
Related Articles
Mobile and tablet compatibility with Scrimba

title: Linking your Scrimba account to Discord
description: Join our Discord at scrimba.com/discord Our Discord server is a thriving hub of students, industry professionals, and the Scrimba team, all coming together to l
body:
Support Home
Linking your Scrimba account to Discord

Join our Discord at scrimba.com/discord
Our Discord server is a thriving hub of students, industry professionals, and the Scrimba team, all coming together to learn, share, and connect.

Here’s how to link your Discord account to Scrimba and unlock the exclusive Pro role if you’re a Pro Member:

First, click on the dropdown at the top left of our server
Then in the dropdown menu, click on Linked Roles:
Then choose the role to link:
Then click Scrimba V2:
A new browser window should pop up. Click Authorize:
Then, back in discord, it should say that Scrimba V2 has connected. Click done:
Then click Finish:
Lastly, make sure you have "Show All Channels" checked in your server settings. This settings menu can be accessed by clicking the arrow icon next to our server name.
Last updated on December 19, 2025

body:

My progress is not correct, how do I fix it?
If your progress isn’t saving correctly, or you’d like to manually mark a lesson or module as complete, follow the steps below:
Marking a single lesson as complete
On the main course page, right click the lesson title on the main lesson listing page
Click Mark Done
Marking an entire module as complete
One the main course page, right click the module section you want to complete
Click Mark Module Done

title: How do I get a refund?
description: Submit a refund request: https://form.typeform.com/to/PJFkZVF7 Full refunds are available if requested within 7 days of the initial purchase, with a limit of on
body:
Support Home
How do I get a refund?
Submit a refund request: https://form.typeform.com/to/PJFkZVF7
Full refunds are available if requested within 7 days of the initial purchase, with a limit of one refund per account. We do not offer pro-rated refunds or refunds for subscription renewals. Refunds are issued based on the amount received, excluding any additional charges such as bank or exchange fees.

If you require further assistance with your refund request please contact us on help@scrimba.com.

title: How to sign in/sign up
description: To sign in, follow these steps: Click the “Sign In” button in the menu on the left side of the page. Choose to authenticate with GitHub or email. If signing in
body:
Support Home
How to sign in/sign up
To sign in, follow these steps:
Click the “Sign In” button in the menu on the left side of the page.
Choose to authenticate with GitHub or email.
If signing in with email, check your inbox for a confirmation email (we use a passwordless sign-in process).
If you don’t see the email, check your spam/junk folder.
Click the confirmation link in the email to complete the sign-in process.

Signing in and signing up follow the same process. If you don’t have an account, one will be created automatically. If your account already exists, you’ll be signed in.
Last updated on February 4, 2025

title: How do free challenges work?
description: Non-Pro users have 10 free challenge slots on our platform. What Are Challenges? Challenges are interactive points within a lesson where the lesson pauses and e
body:
Support Home
How do free challenges work?
Non-Pro users have 10 free challenge slots on our platform.
What Are Challenges?

Challenges are interactive points within a lesson where the lesson pauses and enters interactivity mode, marked by an alien icon. These are typically found in challenge sections of courses.

How the Quota Works:
If you interact with a challenge by adding your own code and saving your solution, it counts toward your 10 free challenge limit.
The 10 free challenges apply across all courses and your entire account.
Once you reach your max quota, you can skip challenge points and continue watching lessons without interruption.
The free challenges quota does not reset. Once you reach this limit, you will need to become a Pro member to continue accessing these challenges.

Can I still continue watching the free lesson?
If you have reached your free challenges limit, you can still continue with the lesson if it's free. If you are prompted about your challenge meeting the free threshold, you can skip this message via the skip button in the top menu bar.
Last updated on February 14, 2025

title: I'm having problems subscribing
description: There could be many reasons why your card payment is being declined. Follow the steps below to troubleshoot this issue: 1. Make sure that you have enough funds
body:
Support Home
I'm having problems subscribing
There could be many reasons why your card payment is being declined. Follow the steps below to troubleshoot this issue:

1. Make sure that you have enough funds in your account
The most common reason for rejected charges is insufficient funds. Please verify that your account has enough funds to complete the transaction. Additionally, be aware that your bank may charge extra fees on top of our subscription price. Kindly take these potential additional costs into consideration.

2. Try a shorter plan (to get a lower transaction amount)
The higher the transaction amount, the more likely it is that banks may reject it. If you are unable to purchase our Annual plan (12 months), please try purchasing our Monthly plan instead.

3. Try another card (e.g. credit card instead of a debit card)
If you have multiple cards, please consider using a different card for the transaction. Customers often report that while one card was rejected, another was successful. This is particularly common with debit cards, which tend to have higher decline rates from banks.

4. Clear your browser cache
Sometimes clearing the cache can resolve issues with the payment process. Please clear your browser’s cache and cookies, then attempt the transaction again.

5. Try it on another browser
Some browsers may interfere with our payment flow, so we recommend trying it on another browser just to see if this could be the issue. If you have browser extensions installed, we recommend using a private window with the extensions disabled too, as this can also interfere with the payment flow.

6. Reach out to your bank to accept international transactions
If you have tried all the previous steps, please contact your bank and request them to enable international transactions on your card. This is often the reason for payment blocks. Additionally, even if international payments are enabled, your bank may be blocking Scrimba as an unrecognized vendor or there might be another restriction.

7. Reach out to us
If you have tried all of the above steps and your payment is still being declined, please reach out to us at help@scrimba.com. We'll check and see what else we can do to assist you.
Last updated on February 5, 2025

Scrimba Pro vs. Free Membership
What do I get on the Pro plan?
Get full access to all current and future premium courses.
Join our private Discord community to connect with industry professionals, mentors, and like-minded students.
Enjoy unlimited access to interactive challenges.
What do I get in the free plan?

title: The editor is not working correctly
description: If your code won’t run or you encounter a sync error, TypeError, or other error message issues, follow these troubleshooting steps: Double check your code Synta
body:
Support Home
The editor is not working correctly
If your code won’t run or you encounter a sync error, TypeError, or other error message issues, follow these troubleshooting steps:

Double check your code
Syntax errors, infinite loops, and other issues can cause unwanted behavior in our editor.

Use Incognito Mode
Open our platform in a private/incognito window. Some browser extensions interfere with functionality, so this helps minimize issues.

Create a Dedicated Browser Profile
Instead of uninstalling extensions, set up a separate user profile for Scrimba in your browser. Learn how to do this in Chrome here: https://support.google.com/chrome/answer/2364824

Try a Different Browser
Our platform works best on Chromium-based browsers (such as Chrome and Edge). While Safari and Firefox may work for some, they can cause compatibility issues.

Restart Your Browser
Close all browser windows completely, then reopen and try again.

Clear Your Cache
Cached files can sometimes cause errors. Clear your browser cache and refresh the page.

Restart Your Device
A full device restart can resolve temporary system issues affecting your browser.

Test on Another Device or Network
If possible, try accessing our platform from another device or a different internet network. In rare cases, network restrictions can cause errors.

If none of these steps resolve the issue, contact our support team at https://scrimba.com/help
Last updated on April 1, 2025

title: How to I reset my progress?
description: To reset your progress for a course, right-click the course title, open the context menu, and select "Reset Progress." Please note that this process only resets
body:
Support Home
How to I reset my progress?
To reset your progress for a course, right-click the course title, open the context menu, and select "Reset Progress."

Please note that this process only resets your progress number; it does not delete your challenge submissions. To delete these as well, you will need to right-click the challenge icon and click "Reset Challenge," which will remove your submission. Currently, there is no bulk option to do this, so it will need to be done per lesson as you proceed.

Please note that these actions is irreversible.
Last updated on April 23, 2025

title: What is Instant Feedback?
description: Our instant feedback feature lets students check their challenge solutions in real time to get insights and verify if their code submission is correct. Teachers
body:
Support Home
What is Instant Feedback?
Our instant feedback feature lets students check their challenge solutions in real time to get insights and verify if their code submission is correct. Teachers typically review the solution and walk through the reasoning behind it for clarity. If you experience any issues with instant feedback, such as incorrect error messages or other problems, you can submit a dispute directly from the error prompt.

More information about can be found on our blog article here: https://v1.scrimba.com/articles/instant-feedback/
Last updated on February 17, 2025

title: Can I pay using PayPal, UPI or others?
description: Our payment processor supports various payment methods based on your local currency. Different currencies allow different payment types, and you’ll see your ava
body:
Support Home
Can I pay using PayPal, UPI or others?
Our payment processor supports various payment methods based on your local currency. Different currencies allow different payment types, and you’ll see your available options at checkout. To view a full breakdown of supported payment methods, check out this article: Stripe Payment Methods Overview.

PayPal is a popular payment method, but it’s only available for these currencies:
EUR, GBP, USD, CHF, CZK, DKK, NOK, PLN, SEK, AUD, CAD, HKD, NZD, SGD.

At this time, we do not support UPI.
Last updated on February 20, 2025
Related Articles

My automatic payment failed. What do I do?

title: How do I delete my account?
description: To delete your account, click your profile icon in the top left to open your account page. Go to the Settings tab, scroll to the bottom, and click the red “Dele
body:
Support Home
How do I delete my account?
To delete your account, click your profile icon in the top left to open your account page. Go to the Settings tab, scroll to the bottom, and click the red “Delete Account” button to begin the deletion process.

⚠ Account deletions are permanent and cannot be undone.
Last updated on March 1, 2025

title: How do I copy a scrim?
description: If you're watching a scrim and want to copy the entire scrim codebase and assets, you can use our "remix" feature. We have a feature called "remix," which is th
body:
Support Home
How do I copy a scrim?
If you're watching a scrim and want to copy the entire scrim codebase and assets, you can use our "remix" feature. We have a feature called "remix," which is the way to fork/copy scrims to your account. To remix a scrim, right-click on the navigation bar to open the context menu, and click "remix." This will create a copy of the scrim you're watching in your account.
Last updated on July 25, 2025

title: What is Scrimbassadors?
description: The Scrimbassadors program lets you earn 30% sales commission from every payment during their first 12 months as a subscriber. on every subscription you refer.
body:
Support Home
What is Scrimbassadors?
The Scrimbassadors program lets you earn 30% sales commission from every payment during their first 12 months as a subscriber. on every subscription you refer.
Your friend(s) also get an extra 20% off the learner subscription plan (Currently called Pro)
More information can be found at https://scrimba.com/scrimbassadors
Last updated on December 2, 2025
